WSE Training Batches
About

Biography

Roles & Identities
  • Practicing Christian
  • Crusader of Purity
  • Paediatric Oncologist & Child Health Specialist
  • Teacher & Youth Counselor
  • Author — 36+ Published Volumes
  • Founder, CASRHA Foundation
  • Convener, WSE Monthly Training
  • Youth Leader, Deeper Life Bible Church

Dr. John S. Balogun is a prominent Nigerian Paediatric Oncologist and Child Health Specialist based in Abuja, Nigeria. He is highly recognized for both his specialized medical work in childhood cancer and his extensive advocacy in youth mentorship, relationships, and sex education.

Dr. Balogun has a comprehensive clinical background focused on paediatric health. He completed his clinical foundation and paediatric medical residency training at the University of Abuja Teaching Hospital (UATH), advancing through post-graduate medical pathways under the West African College of Physicians. To specialize in childhood cancers, he undertook advanced oncology clinical fellowships at Korle Bu Teaching Hospital in Accra, Ghana, and the world-renowned Tata Memorial Centre in Mumbai, India.

He maintains active membership in prestigious global medical bodies, including the International Society of Paediatric Oncology (SIOP) and the American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO).

Passionately involved in child and adolescent education for close to two decades, Dr. Balogun has been mentoring youths since he was 19 years old, a calling that comes very naturally to him. He is a seasoned, gifted teacher of God's word and a sexual purity crusader with a certification in Pragmatic Approaches for Youth and Adolescent Sexual and Reproductive Health. He uses every opportunity to reach out and help people to know the Lord Jesus Christ, without pursuit of prize or reward. He worships with Deeper Life Bible Church and currently serves as the Youth Leader of the church's Quarters Group of Districts in Gwagwalada, FCT, Nigeria.

Early Life and Education

Dr. Balogun was born in Ondo State, Nigeria. He grew up as the 5th of 14 children in a polygamous family, being the first son and the 4th of 8 children born to his mother. He started living alone at the age of 11 due to his mother's decision to ensure he obtained a quality education that was unavailable in their village.

His academic dedication bore fruit when he graduated as the best graduating student from Beulah Baptist High School in Ore. He went on to earn his Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery degrees from Nnamdi Azikiwe University in Anambra State, where he was a beneficiary of the MTN Foundation Science and Technology Scholarship throughout his university years.

Throughout his life, Dr. Balogun has lived in various parts of Nigeria, including Ore in Ondo State; Omu-Aran and Offa in Kwara State; and Awka and Nnewi in Anambra State. He currently resides in Abuja. While he has lived only in Nigeria, his travels have taken him to other African and Asian nations, including visits to Addis Ababa, New Delhi, and Chennai.

Advocacy and Foundations

Dr. Balogun is a Crusader of Sexual Purity, a vehicle he and his wife use to teach age-appropriate, culturally relevant, and scientifically accurate sex education. He is the convener of the monthly Wholesome Sex Education Training for children's and youth teachers, parents, and child advocates.

Together, they founded the CASRHA Foundation, an organization dedicated to promoting Wholesome Sex Education and raising sexual and reproductive health awareness in schools, children's and youth camps, campuses, and religious organizations. More information about their activities can be found at https://wse.casrhafoundation.org. Additionally, they host a monthly neighborhood meeting tagged Hangout with Dr. & Mrs. BALOGUN, where they host preteens, teens, and young adults for discussions on sex education.

Literary Work and Creative Interests

As a prolific writer, Dr. Balogun prefers writing to speaking, though he notes that when pushed to the podium, he can speak extensively. He writes mostly on Christian living, the possibility of premarital and marital sexual purity, relationships, and marriage.

To the glory of God, he has written over 30 books. His publications can be accessed at www.bjseyi.wordpress.com/downloads. He is a voracious reader who prefers digital books to hard copies, and he learns both actively and passively from the world around him. He also composes songs and currently considers his children as his team members, with plans to release family albums in the future.

Personal Life and Faith

Dr. Balogun describes his temperament as a Sanguine with a phlegmatic touch, noting that his character has been thoroughly refined by his encounter with the Person of Jesus Christ, whose life he now lives. He has learned to carry the presence of God everywhere he goes, which helps him maintain the freshness of the Spirit at all times. His favorite books of the Bible are the Gospel of John, the three epistles of John, and the Epistle to the Ephesians.

He is married to Opeyemi, a children's and youth teacher, professional counselor, and one of his former chemistry students from senior secondary school. They are blessed with two adorable children.

"God gave me a mandate, and that is what I'm running with. Anyone who runs with me, I cannot promise you anything, but I can tell you that you can never regret doing this work with me."
— Dr. John S. Balogun
In His Own Words

High-Impact Statements

On Executive Work/Life Strategy & Purpose

"In all sincerity, since I've been serving God, I've never achieved a balance. There's always that adjusting, adjusting, adjusting, adjusting to make sure that nothing is left unattended to. We just make sure that there is no collapse."

"My motto since I was a medical student has been Romans 12:11: Not slothful in business, fervent in spirit, serving the Lord."

"Once I go to work, I put in everything... such that when any patient meets me, they will not have met a better doctor. When any colleague meets me, they will not have met a better colleague."

"The moment I put my head on the pillow, I sleep. Why? Because I'm a very happy man. I sleep very happy every day because I feel like I'm living a life of fulfillment, living the way God wants me to live."

On Advocating for Wholesome Sex Education

"People are accepting ignorance over exposure. They feel it's better to keep their children in the dark... they do not know that the silence and ignorance you try to accept will only be filled up by the internet."

"The internet does not have a moral compass. The internet cannot tell the difference between the right and the wrong. It is you, the parent, who must be the primary source."

"When you learn the correct way to teach age-appropriate sex education, you discover that the fear of exposure will completely disappear."

On Core Mandate & Divine Partnership

"God gave me a mandate, and that is what I'm running with. Anyone who runs with me, I cannot promise you anything, but I can tell you that you can never regret doing this work with me."

Exclusive

Birthday Interview

Interview with Dr. John S. Balogun

A candid, unscripted conversation on impact, purpose, faith, and a 21-year legacy.

Date June 6, 2026
Interviewers Surv. Emmanuel Bayode (Media Team Lead) & Nurse Damilola Ajibade (Content Team Head)
Celebrant / Interviewee Dr. John S. Balogun
Introductory Segment
Surv. Emmanuel:
"Let me start by introducing you. We are happy to meet you, actually; we are very excited, and I believe that everyone who will be watching this will be excited for this particular video. It is exciting that we have the honor of our mentor, our father, and I'll say he's a practicing Christian, he's also a medical doctor, he's the convener of Wholesome Sex Education, and I'm also excited that I'm meeting... I mean, myself and Sister Dami, we are meeting this person who is behind the monthly training. He's the founder of CASRHA Foundation. And with us is Dr. John S. Balogun. Good to have you here, sir. Please let us meet you."
Dr. John S. Balogun:
"Thank you very much for having me, though I don't know why I'm here, but I just know that I'm in a safe space. So..."
Surv. Emmanuel:
"You're in a safe space, sir."
Dr. John S. Balogun:
"Okay, all right. So, just like you have said, I'm John. I'm happy to be here. Thank you."
Surv. Emmanuel:
"All right, thank you very much, sir. Sir, permit me to also introduce myself. I'm Surveyor Emmanuel Bayode, and I'm the media team lead of CASRHA Foundation. With me is Nurse Damilola Ajibade, and she is our content creator team head of the same foundation. Together, we'll be having this interview with Dr. John. Sister Dami, please over to you."
Nurse Damilola:
"All right, thank you so much, sir, for that introduction. Dr. John, we are excited to have you here today. Thank you so much for honoring our invite, even though it's at short notice. This is an interview session for you, and it's going to be a brief one. I know you were not informed of the questions, however, and that's because we really want to get into your heart at the moment, to know what you think about all of those questions right now. Our questions are in four segments. I'll be taking the first segment. My partner, Surveyor Emmanuel, will take the second segment. I'll pick up from there to take the third segment, and he will take the last segment. So, sir, are you ready? Can I proceed?"
Dr. John S. Balogun:
"Well, I don't know whether I'm ready. When the questions start coming, then I will be able to establish whether I'm ready or not. It's okay."
Nurse Damilola:
"Don't worry, there are questions that you can answer..."
1
Scope & Impact Metrics
Led by Nurse Damilola Ajibade
Q
Nurse Damilola Ajibade
Looking back over nearly two decades that you've been actively involved in children and adolescent sex education, teaching and raising sexual puritans; if you were to estimate very conservatively, I know that because we did not inform you ahead of time you may not be able to give the exact or accurate figures, and that may even take you a long time to calculate, but we want to have an estimate of what the bigger picture looks like. The question is, if you were to estimate conservatively, how many schools, campuses, youth camps, or outreaches do you think we have brought the sexual purity crusade to under the Foundation? This includes not just yourself alone, but people you have raised, parents, teachers, team members, and facilitators working with you. Can you give us a rough estimate of the number of schools and places that we have gone to to teach about sexual purity? Thank you, sir.
A
Dr. John S. Balogun

Yeah, thank you very much for that question. It's a very tough one. That question is tough, and I'm happy that you are able to really know that it is tough. Well, it will be difficult to give an estimate. It is just like asking how many people Jesus Christ has saved since He gave His life for humanity. Only heaven can tell that. By God's grace, I started this outreach at the age of 19, and by next month, July 20th, I will be 40. So I've been involved in this work for 21 years, to the glory of God. Every year, there's always a new milestone, new territories, new campuses, new schools, and new churches being reached with the message of wholesome sex education.

So it's difficult to really tell because, apart from the fact that we have been able to train so many teachers and parents, and many of them are carrying on that assignment, the people who were taught are also teaching other people. Everybody is reaching out from different parts of the world, not just in Nigeria. It's difficult to really tell exactly the extent of all of this work. The fact that the work is also being conducted on the internet makes the reach difficult to estimate. Once it's on the internet, it means that even while we are sleeping, the work is still being done in different places of the world. So it's really difficult.

There are some places that I've never been to in my life, however, my voice has been there, and my picture has been there. I've connected to several churches, campuses, and schools over social media through Google Meet, Zoom, and different media platforms. I cannot really say this is the exact estimate, but I know that as far as God is concerned, God knows this estimate. For me, I have taken my mind off trying to track exact numbers. Rather, I'm focused on the impact. I'm focused on who, what, and where I can impact more lives. Whatever has been done, I keep it behind me, and then I reach out to new territories to see what else we can do to expand the work so that more people can be blessed. I hope I have tried to answer your very tough question. You told me that the questions would be easy for me to answer.

Q
Nurse Damilola Ajibade
All right, thank you so much, sir. I think that's a beautiful way to approach my questions regarding numbers. Thank you so much for answering it beautifully. My next question, which is the last for this segment, is also a numbers question, however, I will not frame it around strict numbers. I would just like to proceed and continue from where you stopped talking about impact. I would like to ask you, sir, in terms of pure impact, what can you say of the impact this training has had on students, teachers, parents, and every advocate of children who has come across it? As at the time we are having this interview on the 6th of June 2026, it is interesting to note that on this day, 6/6/2026, we are currently having the registration ongoing for the 43rd badge of the monthly wholesome sex education. Based on the past wholesome sex education monthly trainings, what can you share about their impact? Thank you, sir.
A
Dr. John S. Balogun

Thank you so much. That is another very tough question because only God can really tell objectively the amount of impact whatever we do is able to generate. We can only assume. Maybe you see somebody responding to what you have said, but people's responses are not enough to judge what your impacts are. Let me just quickly deviate a little bit. There's a story of a pastor who was preaching in a church, and a lady was crying profusely. When the pastor saw that the lady was crying profusely, he started hammering the point even more. The pastor felt that what he was saying was entering this woman very well, so he kept talking and hammering the point. At the end of the service, the pastor invited the lady with excitement, saying, 'Oh lady, I see the Lord was touching you.' The pastor thought that he was making an impact, not knowing that the lady had recently lost her dog, and the dog looked exactly like the pastor!

That is to tell you that what we try to do, by the grace of God, is to make sure that the Scripture, the Bible, is our focus. When whatever we do has scriptural backing and we pray over it, we make sure that we don't just go and teach the people with human wisdom. Since we have put all of those things in place, we believe that whatever impact we are seeing is genuine impact. When we see people giving testimonies, we know that it is God changing their lives, not because of any effort that we are putting into whatever we are doing. With that, I still want to say that it is only God who can truly measure the impact.

However, since we are revolving around numbers, I can concretely say by the grace of God that the past episodes of the training have been very impactful based on some of the results we have seen. Take the two of you, for instance. The only reason why you came to meet me, the only way you knew me, was through the CASRHA Foundation. The CASRHA Foundation is about teaching sexual and reproductive health, creating awareness about it, and empowering parents, children, and adolescents to be able to understand their sexuality and protect themselves. I mean not just themselves, but also protect the people around them and protect their future, so that anything they do today is not going to contaminate or destroy the destiny that they are building for themselves.

Look at when we started this training. I remember batch one, I was the only facilitator. Batch two, I was the only facilitator. It was in batch three that, by God's grace, my wife came on board, and when she came on board, she also went through the training. I took my time to brief her and to take her through every aspect of the training, and then she got involved. I think by batch four or five or so, that was when Surv. Emmanuel came on board as a facilitator after he had taken the training. He became a facilitator, and that is what the Bible says in the book of Second Timothy chapter 2, verse two. It says the things you have learned of me, commit them unto faithful men who also will be able to teach others. That is the pattern we are following at CASRHA Foundation. We empower people to the point that they will be strong enough to empower other people, and that is how we keep passing it on.

That is why it is difficult to really tell the full extent of the impact, because one person you impact also goes ahead to impact another person, which is exactly what is happening. Look at the two of you now; both of you are facilitators. Currently, we have six facilitators and we are expanding. New facilitators are on board participating in the training from different parts of the world, meaning different countries, and they are representing CASRHA Foundation where they are. All we need to do is to just give them the go-ahead, give them the support, and then we see that they are representing us well, teaching there. There is impact, but my prayer is that every one of us, including myself, will be able to make it to heaven on the last day, so when we get there and we meet our Jesus face to face, we'll be able to see how much impact His grace in our lives has made. Thank you very much.

2
The Clinical-Faith Hybrid & Medi-Trick
Led by Surv. Emmanuel Bayode
Q
Surv. Emmanuel Bayode
All right, thank you very much. The next question is about you being certified in pragmatic approaches to adolescent reproductive health, but you are also a pediatrician, dealing with children, and a teacher of God's word. How do you balance clinical science with the spiritual, even within your family? How are you balancing the whole thing and speaking with youth? I know there was a time you told me that you are coordinating youths. How are you able to balance all of these things along with your work? We really want to learn from that, sir.
A
Dr. John S. Balogun

Yeah, thank you very much. Let me say that that is a very, very important question to ask, and I'm happy to talk about this. Let me quickly say here in all sincerity, since I've been serving God, I've never achieved a perfect balance. I'm sure these are some of the truths that most people would not want to tell. You know, when people are on air like this, they want to show that it is just the grace of God, grace of God, grace of God. I have never once achieved a balance at any point in time where everything was just perfect. There has never been that time. But what happens is that when we get involved in a particular area, we recognize that there are other areas that need attention, and then we intentionally create time to make up for those areas. We might not keep a steady balance, but we just make sure that there is no collapse.

There is this movement, I don't know if you have seen the way a scale moves like this, so there's no time that is perfect and everything is standing completely still. There's always that adjusting, adjusting, adjusting, adjusting to make sure that nothing is left unattended to.

Now, let me proceed by saying that my motto since I was a medical student has been Romans chapter 12, verse 11. The motto is this: 'Not slothful in business; fervent in spirit; serving the Lord.' You can see there are three things there. Number one, I have a business, which is what gives me my daily or monthly income, the work I do that I use to bring food to my table. I'm not slothful; I make sure I'm not slothful in that. The second one is being fervent in spirit, making sure that I pay attention to my personal relationship with God, making sure that my spirit is always warm, always hot for God. My love for God does not dwindle, it is always hot, so that is number two. The third aspect is serving the Lord, and that is where all of these other services come in, either in the local church or the service to humanity we do through the CASRHA Foundation.

I have all of this before me and I say, okay, I have my personal relationship with God to cater for, I have my personal job, the one that gives me money, to cater for, and then I have a lot of people to attend to. I try to bring in all of this, and at the beginning of every year, I try to set goals for myself. I set a goal: what are the things I want to achieve for my personal life? What are the things I want to achieve for my career? What are the things I want to achieve for my service to humanity? All of these.

For those of you who have been close to me, you will discover that all our activity for CASRHA Foundation for the whole of 2026 was already set out as at December last year. The activity for this year was already scheduled from January to December 2026 with all the dates. All our activities, everything was already set out. Then I communicate with you all, my team members, saying, 'Okay, these are the things we need to do,' and I delegate, 'Okay, you are going to handle this, and you are going to handle this.' Then I stand in the back seat and watch all of you carry out those things that we have discussed. I try not to interfere as much as possible so that I can allow you all to also grow, be able to make decisions, and build your own leadership capacity while I'm serving as a moderator. If you need my input at any point, I can moderate whatever you are doing. With that, the work is still being done. At any point you need me to be involved, I know that you are going to get me involved. Look at this interview you conducted; it wasn't even part of our CASRHA Foundation plan, but you came up with this and said we need to do this interview, and here we are.

When it comes to my personal relationship with God, I also factor that in. I plan how many times I will wait upon the Lord in a month or in a week, and all of that. I put it in place, and I plan which passage of the Bible I will read for the month of January, February, and so on. I already put all of that in place so that when I wake up in the morning, I'm not thinking, 'Oh, where should I read?' I already structure all of that out. For the mentorship, I have a dedicated email address where I conduct mentorship for several people from all walks of life, I call it my mentorship email, and that is where I interact with my mentees from time to time.

Regarding my career, once I go to work, I put in everything I need to put into my work, such that when any patient meets me, they will not have met a better doctor. When any colleague meets me, they will not have met a better colleague. When my bosses meet me, they would not have met a better subordinate. That is the way I put in all of this energy so that at the end of the day, the moment I put my head on the pillow, I sleep. Why? Because I'm a very happy man. I sleep very happy every day because I feel like I'm living the way God wants me to live. I'm living a life of fulfillment, and that makes me sleep easily. Nothing bothers me. If anything wants to bother me, I just commit it to God in prayer and then I sleep off. These are ways, these are some of the things I do that have helped me, like I said, not to keep a perfect balance, there is no balance, but at least we make sure that nothing is falling. Just gather everything. If anybody tells you that they have everything balanced out, you may need to go and interview them too to learn their way. For me, since I've started doing this, I've never achieved that balance, but I can say by the grace of God, things have not fallen apart. Thank you.

Q
Surv. Emmanuel Bayode
Thank you, thank you very much, sir. In fact, it seems that each time we meet you, we have to be educated, like you have a lot of value to add to us. As much as we are having this interview, I am learning myself, and I believe that everyone watching is also learning. It's so wonderful to have you once again. Yes, so we would like to ask, what is the absolute biggest misconception when we are talking about sex education? What is the absolute biggest misconception people have about sex education, and how does the approach from CASRHA Foundation fix it? Thank you, sir.
A
Dr. John S. Balogun

Thank you very much. Yes, the question is very clear. Let me say what the biggest misconception is: people accepting ignorance over exposure. They feel it's better to keep their children in the dark about sex education than for you to tell them and expose them. They think telling them will mean that you are exposing them, and exposing them will mean that they will start practicing it. That's what I mean by accepting ignorance over exposure. They just think, 'Okay, let's just keep our children in the dark, let them not know about this thing so that they will remain pure.' They do not know that the silence and the ignorance you try to accept will be filled up by the internet instead.

If we don't talk to them, and I always say this, the internet does not have a moral compass. The internet cannot tell the difference between the right and the wrong. It is you, the parent, who can tell between the right and the wrong, and that is why they must hear from the parents first. First of all, hear from the parents so that with every other piece of information they hear outside, they will have to bring that information to compare with their parents' opinion and their parents' education. But if they hear that thing first of all on the internet, on the television, or outside, what happens is that anything you as a parent tell them, they will be comparing with what their friends, the internet, or television is saying. I don't think that is what any parent wants for their child.

That is why we are coming out and saying the best time to talk about this thing is when they are growing, when they are small. From two years old, you can begin to talk about their private parts and their public parts, and as they grow, you build on that. When they are growing to seven or eight years old, you start talking about puberty, things that are going to begin to change in their body as they grow. The moment they know that you talk about all of these things to them, when they hear it from outside, they will come back home and say, 'Ah, so-and-so person said this. Mommy, daddy, what do you think?' They want to hear your opinion because you have become the primary source for them.

That is what we are trying to say: parents, you cannot choose ignorance over exposure. Yes, exposure can prompt them to start acting, but that is why we say come in, come and be trained, and come and learn the correct way to teach age-appropriate sex education. When you learn the correct way to teach, you discover that the fear of exposure that you have will disappear. You just realize, 'Oh, so I didn't know it's like this,' and then you'll be able to teach it properly. Training is very important, and with that, we are able to demystify that primary misconception that if you teach them this, they are going to become promiscuous. Thank you.

3
Intellectual Legacy & Literature
Led by Nurse Damilola Ajibade
Q
Nurse Damilola Ajibade
All right, thank you so much. I know we are rounding up, however, I would like to let you know that the conversation is actually getting interesting. We are going into the second half of the interview. This is the third segment now, where we are talking about your books and talking about legacy. We know you have written a lot of volumes, about 30-plus books. If you could pick the top two or top three on sexual purity that parents, teachers, adolescents, or anyone who is passionate about children could read, which ones are they? Which ones would you recommend?
A
Dr. John S. Balogun

Yeah, thank you very much for that question. Yes, truly by God's grace, I've written a number of volumes, and interestingly, there are quite a number of volumes that are not yet online. In fact, there are some that I thought were online. I think after now, I will work with the media team so that we can get the other ones online. I've checked through because the books I've written now should be close to 36, but I checked the online gallery and discovered that we only have 31 online there. We will work together to see how we can upload the other ones.

Now, there are quite a number of volumes on sexual purity and on sex education. For instance, the first one that I wrote was 'Living a Sexually Pure Life'. 'Living a Sexually Pure Life' contains a teaching that I recommend for every young person, including teenagers, young adults, and even parents who have children in those age groups. It is a book that talks about how the grace of God can help a sinner to get back on their feet, especially those who have been involved in sexual sin. In fact, I remember I dedicated that book to people who have messed up, people who have lost course with God because of sexual sin. I wrote that, and I think that would also be very helpful.

Then I've also written one on 'Freedom from Masturbation', and on this, I've gotten a lot of calls and messages from people who are having difficulty overcoming masturbation. I've also written a volume on 'Gaining Mastery Over Your Sexual Urge'; that also is an outline in itself that will help provide the necessary information that every young person needs to be able to guide them to gain mastery over their sexual urge. There are some others like 'Danger of Premarital Sex', which is also for young people. Then there is 'The Blessedness of a Pure Life', which is also one of those contents. There is 'Understanding Your Sexuality'; in 'Understanding Your Sexuality', I talk about practical aspects of puberty. These are things that I think will really help. What I can do is that I may have to create a volume by God's grace, I will see how easy it is going to be for me to create a volume that will be something on sex education for children and adolescents, which will be different from our manual. I think it is necessary to create that volume so that it will be like a compendium for people.

Q
Nurse Damilola Ajibade
My last question for this segment will be: as the CASRHA Foundation moves forward, what is the next core mantra or big vision that is driving the next phase of this impact? Thank you, sir.
A
Dr. John S. Balogun

Yeah, thank you very much. Well, without blinking an eye, the thing we want to follow up on now is a focus on mentorship for the next generation. By God's grace, I'm going to be discussing this because it's going to be the focus of our next Sharpen the Axe Conference. We are going to have parents and teachers together with us, where we go through a three-day conference discussing how we can really mentor the next generation intentionally. That is the next big project that we have ahead. I'm happy that you both are hearing this first from me, so it's going to be an intentional mentorship for the next generation. It's something that we're going to be passionate about. We will see how God helps us to really be committed to that mentorship intentionally for years, as God helps us. Thank you.

4
The Ultimate Success Story
Wrap-Up — Led by Surv. Emmanuel Bayode
Q
Surv. Emmanuel Bayode
The last question, sir, is: what is that one hidden success story, maybe alumnus feedback from a child or parent, that has made you say, 'Yes, this is exactly why I do this'? We've heard this several times on the wholesome education platform, saying that you should not stop what God has started using you for, something like that, and you kept saying it. We want to know, what is that thing that keeps giving you the drive to say, 'Yes, this is exactly why I do this'? Is there any success story that you can mention?
A
Dr. John S. Balogun

Okay, we are going back to the question of impact, right? Well, like I always say, if anybody asks me, 'What is my success story regarding the sex education that I've been teaching for the past 21 years?' I would tell them: my wife. Yes, I met my wife when she was in SS1 in secondary school. She was in SS1, and she was in one of my first sets of students that I was teaching sex education. By the grace of God, she embraced the topic like every other student did, and God helped her. Today, the passion with which my wife teaches sex education, my own passion does not even match hers!

I can say by the grace of God that if there is no other person that this education has impacted, it has impacted my wife. This is a practical encounter. You know Peter was talking about his encounter with Jesus, he said, 'The things which we have heard, which we have seen with our eyes, which we have looked upon, and our hands have handled...' It is practical. Nobody is telling Peter, 'Have you ever had an encounter with Jesus before?' where he might be dreaming. He said, 'We saw it, we touched Him.' In the same way, this impact is beyond me. It is now that some people have caught this vision that way, and they even run with the vision. When I see them like this, I imagine, I say, 'Hey God, may my first not become the last.' I'll just wake up and keep moving like that.

I look at the two of you who are interviewing me to see how passionate you have become about sex education. You are also part of my success story, or let me say our success story. Only God knows how many people that you both, just the two of you here, have also impacted. Like I always say, based on the reference you made to a message I always send at every batch, people should never ignore what God is about to do in their lives. You should never ignore it because when I started, I never knew that it was ever going to get to the point where people would start inviting me from different places of the world just to come and teach this education, with people paying for my flights just to come and teach it. I never thought that day would ever come, but you see, it's happening. It's happening because God usually looks ahead and begins to prepare people.

I heard the day He was talking to me in 2006, well written in my notebook. God was telling me the impact He's going to make regarding this sex education. He just told me to remove focus from other things. That is why you see me focus, apart from parenting and sex education. You rarely see me talking about medical jargon or medical topics; I focus because that is what God wants me to do. Because I heard from God that this is the area He wants me to focus on, and I've remained committed and faithful to that call and that mandate, God has been very faithful. He has been expanding us and giving us wisdom. Sometimes when I finish some sessions, I'll be asking myself, 'Where did this wisdom come from?' Sometimes my wife will just look at me and say, 'Ah, where did you get all of these things from?' I say it's the Holy Spirit and all of that. That is to tell you that I'm not just somebody who is running without a purpose. God gave me a mandate, and that is what I'm running with.

I always say that the backing behind this work is beyond me. Anyone who runs with me, I cannot promise you anything, but I can tell you that you can never regret doing this work with me. All those testimonies abound with those who have been working with us in this, showing that God has never abandoned anyone who has invested their time, money, and treasure in this wholesome education campaign and the awareness that we are creating. Thank you very much, I hope I am able to answer your question.

✦
Final Words, Appreciation & Closing Prayer
Q
Nurse Damilola Ajibade
Yeah, thank you so much, sir. As we close this interview session, what will be your final words to everyone that will be listening to this or watching this?
A
Dr. John S. Balogun — Final Words

Okay, thank you very much. Well, I'm not sure I have any additional thing to say, but I will only say, because this interview does not have a specific title, based on what we have discussed so far, I will say that CASRHA Foundation, the full meaning of which is Children and Adolescent Sexual and Reproductive Health Awareness Foundation, has a goal to create awareness about sexual and reproductive health. Let people know that their body belongs to them. We help young people to ensure that no action of today tampers with their future, and nothing they do today leaves a consequence that will become regrettable for them. This is our goal at CASRHA Foundation.

We also get involved in intentional parenting because we know that without the parents, sex education will not be easy to pass across to our young people. I want to say that parents should not accept ignorance over exposure, rather they should intentionally expose their children to the correct, age-appropriate information about sex education so that the children will remain safe both at the time they are growing up and even when they become teenagers and young adults. Once again, I want to appreciate my interviewers for this impromptu interview. You did not allow me to see the questions before you invited me, but I'm happy that God helped me and I was able to answer the questions. Thank you very much, God bless you.

🙏
Closing Prayer
Led by Dr. John S. Balogun — at the request of Surv. Emmanuel Bayode
A
Dr. John S. Balogun

"Father, in the name of Jesus, we want to appreciate You. Thank You for the interviewers, and thank You for this platform. Lord, we pray that everything that we have discussed today, we soak it with Your anointing. Lord, as the word goes out, let it be an encouragement to people who listen to it, in the name of Jesus.

I pray for my interviewers, that Your grace will increase in their lives, in the name of Jesus. Thank You, Father, for answers to our prayer. Lord, bless us, bless CASRHA Foundation, bless all our children, and bless all our parents, in Jesus' name we have prayed."

Blessed Home

Family & Partnership

He is married to Opeyemi, a children's and youth teacher, professional counselor, and one of his former chemistry students from senior secondary school. They are blessed with two adorable children — a family that lives, ministers, and advances the mission of Wholesome Sex Education together.

Mrs. Opeyemi Balogun

Children's & Youth Teacher · Professional Counselor
Co-Facilitator, Wholesome Sex Education

Dr. John & Mrs. Opeyemi

Partners in Life, Ministry & Purpose

The Balogun Family

A living testament — grace, purity & generational legacy

The Foundation's Story

The Story Behind
Wholesome Sex Education

If I can remember, for more than 20 years now, I have been involved in outreaches to schools, churches, campuses, and so on. And while I was all out there, reaching out to these young people, there was a vacuum I noticed. I discovered that no matter how much we work among the students, if we do not bring the parents on board, if we do not bring the teachers on board, we will not be able to achieve much. Why? Because during school hours, the children, these young people spend eight active hours, an average of eight active hours, in the school with their teachers. And then they spend the remaining hours with their parents, and the majority of the remaining hours, some spend other ones with their friends and so on. But the key players in the life of these children are their parents and then the teachers.

And that was what gave birth to the wholesome sex educational training for parents and teachers. We know that if we can empower parents with the requisite information, knowledge that they need, if you can equip the teachers, we are sure that we will be multiplying the effort that God is helping us to make. And this is the reason why we started this training sometime in 2020, 2022, and here we are today. God is still helping us, because we realize that there are some parents and teachers who do not even know what to teach, let alone teaching these young people. While there are some who know what to teach, but they don't know the correct approach to do the teaching. Some people who know what to teach and know the correct approach, often miss the timing. So, all of this is why we have set up this training, so that you can learn: what to teach, how to teach it, and when to start teaching it.

The Ultimate Success Story

When asked to measure the impact of a twenty one year crusade in sexual purity, Dr. John S. Balogun did not start with the forty three continuous rounds of monthly trainings. He didn't count the thousands of online connections that happened on Zoom or Google Meet, and he also did not bring up figures from the many youth camps, schools, and campuses he has been to.

Rather, he looks nearer, almost like at his own doorstep. He points to his wife, Mrs. Opeyemi Balogun.

"If anybody tells me, 'what is my success story about wholesome sex education that I've been teaching for the past 21 years?' I would tell them, my wife."

Their journey started like, over two decades ago, when the dream for the CASRHA Foundation was still like a burning fire inside a young 19 year old medical student's heart. Dr. John met Opeyemi while she was still a young student in SS1. She sat among his very first set of students, taking in that impartful talk about age-appropriate, wholesome sex education… long before it turned into a world wide digital movement.

She did not only hear the message she really embraced it. She ran with it, and you know over the years, that same shared foundation grew into an unbreakable bond in life, ministry, and purpose.

When the monthly trainings properly kicked off, Dr. John stood alone as the only facilitator for Batch 1 and Batch 2. Then by Batch 3, the spiritual alignment seemed just right as Mommy Opeyemi finally stepped onto the platform beside him.

Today, she isn't just a supporting pillar, more like a driving force that keeps moving. Her background as a children and youth teacher, plus professional counselor, matches up in a clean way with his clinical expertise, and together they create this hybrid model that makes the foundation's work feel entirely, uniquely theirs.

"Today, the passion with which my wife teaches sex education, my own passion doesn't even reach her side! The impact is beyond me. It's practical, we see it, we touch it."

For the CASRHA community, the love story of Dr. John and Mommy Opeyemi is the real proof of the message they share. It serves as a living, breathing testament that when sexual purity and wholesome instruction are built into the rhythm of a life, the outcome becomes an enduring legacy of grace, power, and generational momentum.

Literary Legacy

Books & Publications

Dr. Balogun has written over 36 volumes — with a professional goal to publish his 40th book by his 40th birthday. His works span Christian living, sexual purity, relationships, and marriage. Below are key recommended titles highlighted during the interview.

Living a Sexually Pure Life

Recommended for teenagers, young adults, and parents. Addresses how the grace of God can help restore those who have been involved in sexual sin.

Freedom from Masturbation

Addresses a common struggle among young people, providing guidance and hope for those having difficulty overcoming masturbation.

Gaining Mastery Over Your Sexual Urge

An outline providing the essential information every young person needs to gain mastery over their sexual urge.

Danger of Premarital Sex

A vital resource for young people on the serious consequences of premarital sexual activity.

The Blessedness of a Pure Life

Explores the rewards and blessings that come with choosing and maintaining a life of sexual purity.

Understanding Your Sexuality

Covers practical aspects of puberty and sexuality to help young people understand and navigate their development.
